adds.rationale: NVIDIA's GTC 2025 (March 18, 2025) commercially launched Isaac GR00T N1 humanoid-robot foundation models and the Newton physics engine with Fanuc, ABB, Agility, and Unitree, marking robotics' transition from concept to deployable platform; two first-of-kind robotics ETFs (KOID June 4, HUMN June 26) confirm forming institutional demand; BOTZ fills the only named wave with zero coverage and has $3.4B AUM and eight years of history.

news_evidence.summary: NVIDIA announces Isaac GR00T N1, the first open foundation model for generalized humanoid reasoning, plus Cosmos world models and Isaac simulation, at the GTC 2025 keynote March 18.
NVIDIA and global robotics leaders (Agility, Boston Dynamics, Fanuc, Unitree) announce GTC 2025 partnerships to deploy physical-AI platforms commercially.
Roundhill HUMN begins trading June 26, 2025 (second pure-play robotics ETF after KraneShares KOID June 4); both too new for the 1.5y lookback, making the established BOTZ the clean vehicle.

removes.rationale: The product-tanker rate cycle has likely peaked: BIMCO and Drewry both forecast 2025 softening as fleet expansion outpaces demand, Q1 2025 LR2/MR TCE rates are well below 2024 highs, and Red Sea normalization would collapse the long-haul ton-mile thesis — a cyclical trade past its crest, not a structural position.

news_evidence.summary: BIMCO 2025 outlook: crude tankers tighten only slightly while product-tanker rates soften, with fleet expansion outpacing 2.5-3.5% cargo growth.
Drewry warns product tankers 'battle fleet expansion in 2025' as new deliveries accelerate; any earlier Red Sea normalization adds downside.
Scorpio Tankers Q1 2025: LR2 TCE $29k/day and MR $22k/day — materially below 2024 peaks; Q2 charter extensions at $31k/day cap near-term upside, confirming a rate ceiling.

rationale_overall: Single swap — STNG out, BOTZ in — rotates from a cyclical product-tanker trade whose rate thesis has eroded since the 2024 peak (BIMCO and Drewry forecasting further 2025 softening) into NVIDIA's robotics-platform buildout (Isaac GR00T N1 / Cosmos / Newton announced at GTC March 2025), filling the only named wave with zero coverage. Other holdings intact: NUKZ and CEG reinforced by the June 3 Constellation-Meta 20-year nuclear PPA; RKLB executing on cadence; ITA captures the multi-year defense buildup; MSFT and NVDA anchor AI despite MSFT's June EU/Italy regulatory headwinds. Post-swap covers six named waves — AI, rockets, quantum, nuclear, defense, robotics — with demographics open for a future quarter.
